world_name: World of Darkness - Vampire: The Masquerade
prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG gothic-punk world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: World of Darkness / Vampire: The Masquerade inspired gothic horror map, single huge primary continent.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye nocturnal atlas view of a shadowed modern-gothic world, surrounded by black ocean under a blood moon.
Subject: one enormous primary continent with complex realistic coastline: peninsulas, misty gulfs, bays, capes, narrow straits, broken rocky shores, curved coasts, dark river deltas, inland lakes, and varied landforms. Include gothic city districts, vampire Elysium domains, haunted cemeteries, sewer warrens, cathedral spires, fog-filled parks at midnight, ruined havens, old industrial zones, werewolf-haunted forests, mountain passes, wetlands, tundra, and coastal regions.
Style/medium: gothic vampire masquerade city map expanded to continent scale, noir atmosphere, domain territories marked with non-text sigils, dark illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography, highly detailed.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, full single continent visible with generous black ocean border, large high-resolution atlas feel, clean top-down readability.
Lighting/mood: gothic-punk political horror, eternal night, blood moon overhead, deep shadows, ancient debts, betrayal, masquerade secrecy.
Color palette: charcoal black, desaturated purple, blood red, moonlit silver, fog grey, deep blue-black ocean, muted greens and browns under nocturnal haze.
Materials/textures: black parchment, engraved silver ink coastlines, smoky fog layers, subtle blood-red domain sigils, aged paper grain.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Make the coastline natural, intricate, and realistic rather than a smooth blob.
